# CiCwtch - Code of Conduct
## Repository Standards & Collaboration

<p align="left">
  <a href="https://github.com/features/copilot"><img src="https://img.shields.io/badge/GitHub-181717?style=for-the-badge&logo=github&logoColor=white" alt="GitHub" /></a>
  &nbsp;
  <a href="https://flutter.dev/"><img src="https://img.shields.io/badge/Flutter-02569B?style=for-the-badge&logo=flutter&logoColor=white" alt="Flutter" /></a>
  &nbsp;
  <a href="https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/"><img src="https://img.shields.io/badge/Cloudflare%20Workers-F38020?style=for-the-badge&logo=cloudflare&logoColor=white" alt="Cloudflare Workers" /></a>
</p>

## Our standard

All participation in the CiCwtch project spaces should be respectful, constructive, and professional.

Expected behaviour includes:

- discussing ideas in good faith;
- accepting technical review without hostility;
- focusing on facts, evidence, and practical outcomes;
- avoiding harassment, abuse, discrimination, or intimidation.

## Unacceptable behaviour

This includes, but is not limited to:

- personal attacks;
- discriminatory or hateful language;
- deliberate disruption, trolling, or intimidation;
- sharing private information without consent;
- repeated bad-faith arguments after direction has been given.

## Enforcement

The maintainer may remove content, limit access, or end collaboration where behaviour undermines the project, contributor safety, or architectural integrity.

## Reporting

Report concerns through the project support channels listed in `.github/SUPPORT.md`.
